page: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU family server configured with an Intel x86_64 processor providing 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs. The system operates with a single thread per core, indicating that hyperthreading is disabled. It features 180.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 11.25 GB per core.

This server includes a dedicated NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, equipped with 48 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, which supports high-speed read and write operations directly on the host. Additionally, the instance is provisioned with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

A key architectural tradeoff of this instance is its shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce resource contention under sustained CPU-bound workloads. Furthermore, the CPU configuration does not expose advanced instruction sets or hardware features, such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX-512), Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), hardware-assisted cryptography (AES, SHA), or hardware virtualization capabilities.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high ratio of GPU VRAM and system memory relative to the virtual core count, making it a dense platform for memory-heavy operations without requiring a larger CPU footprint. This configuration is suitable for workloads such as large language model inference, graphics rendering, machine learning training, and data science tasks that require substantial GPU memory and local NVMe storage.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU instance designed for memory-intensive accelerated workloads. It features an Intel x86_64 processor with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs under a shared allocation model, with hyperthreading disabled. The hardware configuration includes 180.0 GB of system memory, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM based on the Ada Lovelace architecture. A notable tradeoff is the absence of advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX, AMX, and hardware cryptography. This server provides high resource density, offering a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for tasks requiring substantial GPU memory relative to CPU compute. It is suited for workloads such as machine learning inference, data science, and graphics rendering.
og_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 16 Intel vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for memory-intensive accelerated workloads.

tagline: An accelerated cloud instance featuring a 48 GB NVIDIA L40S GPU, 180 GB RAM, and 1200 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.

16 Intel physical cores and vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
180.0 GB of system memory providing 11.25 GB per core
1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Best-fit for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and data science
